one_line: Warm off-white + serif display + serif body (reader-first). Single column, big margins, no chrome. Feels like Substack, Read.cv, personal essay sites.

background.role: Warm parchment. Slightly warmer than editorial-premium.

surface.role: Card surfaces, rare in this profile.

text_primary.role: Warm dark brown — almost-black with brown undertone. The signature reading color.

accent.role: Burnt orange — used for links, ONLY links. The single accent.

highlight.role: Editor highlights (rare — for callout pull-quotes only).

rules: Light mode only. Dark-mode reading apps are valid, but this profile's identity is the warm parchment
Background is WARM cream (#FBF8F1), not pure white. The whole profile is about reader comfort
Single accent — burnt orange for links. Nothing else gets color
Text is brown-tinged (#2C2418), not pure black. Pure black reads cold and clinical for long-form

display.family: Lyon Display, Charter, Tiempos Headline, Georgia, serif
display.rule: Display serif. Lora is the closest free Google font. Substack itself uses a serif similar to Lyon.

body.family: Charter, Iowan Old Style, Source Serif Pro, Georgia, serif
use_for: BODY TEXT — yes, serif body. This is the defining feature of this profile.
body.rule: THIS IS THE KEY DIFFERENTIATION. Body in serif. Reading apps and long-form publications use serif body because it's easier to read at length. Inter body would put this in editorial-premium territory.

use_for: UI chrome only — author byline metadata, date stamps, navigation, buttons. Anything that isn't prose.

scale.$rule: Base 19px — substantially larger than other profiles. Reading at 19px in serif feels generous and editorial. Substack itself uses ~20px body.

rules: Body in SERIF. Inter is for UI chrome (date, nav, byline) only
Base 19px — bigger than you think it should be. Reading happens
Line-height 1.7-1.8 for prose. Generous breathing room is the design
Pull quotes: italic serif, slightly larger (xl-2xl), indented with a left border
Content max-width: 640-680px. NARROWER than docs (which use 720-800px). Reading comfort is tighter.
TINY radius. Almost everything is 0-4px. This profile is editorial/print-feel.
Zero shadows. Editorial design uses borders + spacing, never shadows
No card surfaces in the reading area. The page background IS the surface

byline.rule: Author name + date appear above article title in sans (Inter), small (sm), text_secondary. Photo optional, small (32-40px) circular avatar.

pull_quote.rule: Indented from left with a thin border. NEVER centered with quotation marks at huge size.

link.rule: Links underlined. Always. The whole web of reading depends on visible link affordance.

button_primary.rule: Rare in this profile. When needed: sans (UI font), small, restrained.

footnote.rule: Footnotes are tiny serif at the bottom of the article. Hover or click to reveal.

article.structure: Single column, max-width 640-680px, centered. Title at top in serif display weight 500-600 (3xl-4xl). Byline below in sans. Then body content in serif, line-height 1.7.
do: Author photo + name + date in sans above title
Dropcap on first paragraph (optional, but it's THE move)
Pull quotes inset with left border
dont: Sidebars, related-articles widgets in the middle of the article

publication_home.structure: Title of publication at top (display serif, large). Below: stacked article cards, each with title (display serif xl) + 2-line excerpt + author + date.
do: Cards separated by 48px gaps, not borders

writer_profile.structure: Top: large circular avatar (96-120px), name in display serif, ~2-line bio in body serif, social links in sans. Below: list of articles.

patterns.article_load: 300ms fade-in on first page load, that's the entire animation budget for the article view.
patterns.image_load: Blur-up effect on inline article images (small placeholder loads first, full image fades in).

anti_patterns: EVERY OTHER ANIMATION PATTERN. Reading interfaces stay still.
